zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir James Rhodes於1560年12月15日出生在英國越可爾希霍爾時，他的父親Thomas Rhodes39歲，母親Alice Moreton33歲。他於1577年與Rosalyn Wilson在英國越可爾哈利法可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1644年1月4日在他的家鄉享年83歲，埋葬在英國越可爾羅伊斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir James Rhodes于1560年12月15日出生在英国越可爾希霍爾时，他的父亲Thomas Rhodes39岁，母亲Alice Moreton33岁。他于1577年与Rosalyn Wilson在英国越可爾哈利法可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1644年1月4日在他的家乡享年83岁，埋葬在英国越可爾羅伊斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Rhodes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: either a topographic name for someone who lived by ‘(the) woodland clearings’, plural form of Middle English rode (Old English rod, rodu), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, principally Rhodes in Bury (Lancashire) or possibly from one of the many minor places in Yorkshire similarly named, or Rhodes Minnis (Kent). The Yorkshire name sometimes alternates with the singular form (see Rhode and Rode ). The Rh- spelling was introduced in the 16th and 17th centuries by clerks with a classical education, who associated the name with the Greek island of Rhodes, famous in ancient history and mythology. There is also no connection with modern English road (Old English rād ‘riding’), which was not used to denote a thoroughfare until the 16th century. The surname is particularly common in Yorkshire and Lancashire but occurs with various spellings in smaller numbers widely across England.
variant of Rhode , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Rhodus
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of Rhodes or German Rhodius, a Latinized form of Rode , perhaps indicating the personal as opposed to the topographic name (see Rode 1).

variants.name: Rod
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Rod, or a topographic name for somene who lived in a woodland clearing, from a derivative of Middle High German roden ‘to clear land for cultivation’. Compare Rhodes .
Norwegian (Rød): habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads so named, from Old Norse ruth ‘clearing’.
Czech and Croatian: from a short form of the Slavic compound personal names based on the element rod ‘family, relatives’, e.g. Rodoslav.

variants.name: Roades
meanings: English: variant of   Rhodes  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Rhode
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English rode (Old English rod, rodu) designating someone who lived by a woodland clearing, or for someone who came from a place so named, such as Road (Somerset), Rhode (Somerset), Road Farm in Colebrook (Devon), Rhode in Halberton (Devon), Roade (Northamptonshire), Rode (Cheshire), and Rodd (Herefordshire).
English: topographic name for someone who lived by a reed bed, from Middle English rode (Old English hrēod). See also Rude and Trude .
German: habitational name from any places called Rhode (Lower Saxony, Rhineland) and Rhoden (Hesse, Saxony-Anhalt, former Silesia).

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir James Rhodes was born on 15 December 1560, in Hipperholme, Yorkshire, England, his father, Thomas Rhodes, was 39 and his mother, Alice Moreton, was 33. He married Rosalyn Wilson in 1577, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 4 January 1644, in his hometown, at the age of 83, and was buried in Royston, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir James Rhodes est né le 15 décembre 1560, à Hipperholme, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Rhodes, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alice Moreton, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Rosalyn Wilson en 1577, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 4 janvier 1644, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterré à Royston, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir James Rhodes am 15. Dezember 1560 in Hipperholme, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Rhodes 39 und seine Mutter Alice Moreton 33. Er hat 1577 in Halifax, Yorkshire, England Rosalyn Wilson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 4. Januar 1644 im Alter von 83 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Royston, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Rhodes è nato il il 15 dicembre 1560 nel Hipperholme, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Rhodes, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Alice Moreton, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Rosalyn Wilson nel 1577, nel Halifax, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 4 gennaio 1644, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolto a Royston, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Rhodesが1560年12月15日にイギリスヨークシュアイアヒップエロールメで生まれた時，父Thomas Rhodesは39歳，母Alice Moretonは33歳であった。1577年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスでRosalyn Wilsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1644年1月4日に故郷で83歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアロイストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Rhodes(이)가 1560년 12월 15일 영국 요르크실르 히p홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Rhodes(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Moreton(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1577년 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 Rosalyn Wilson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1644년 1월 4일 그의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 로이스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.15 December 1560–4 January 1644: 15 de dezembro de 1560–4 de janeiro de 1644
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir James Rhodes nasceu em 15 de dezembro de 1560, em Hipperholme, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Rhodes, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Alice Moreton, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Rosalyn Wilson em 1577, em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 4 de janeiro de 1644, em sua cidade natal, com 83 anos, e foi sepultado em Royston, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir James Rhodes родился 15 декабря 1560 г. в местности хиппиролм, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Rhodes было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Moreton, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Rosalyn Wilson в 1577 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 4 января 1644 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 83 лет и был похоронен в местности роистон, еркшир, Англия.

es.15 December 1560–4 January 1644: 15 de diciembre de 1560–4 de enero de 1644
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir James Rhodes nació el 15 de diciembre de 1560, en Hipperholme, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Rhodes, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Alice Moreton, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Rosalyn Wilson en 1577, en Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 4 de enero de 1644, en su pueblo, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultado en Royston, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
